title: I mapped narrative strength vs stock performance — most AI names aren’t confirming the story
author: topicspace
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/zd12mr9xbesg1.png?width=900&format=png&auto=webp&s=4894b7e7991d780992bb73a900f0192a26797fb7

I’ve been tracking a bunch of AI-related news/events over the past couple months and wanted to sanity check it against price.

So I plotted:

* how strong the narrative is (based on recent activity)
* vs how the stock actually performed relative to QQQ

What stood out:

Most of the names with the *strongest* narratives are still underperforming.

Examples:

* MSFT → tons of enterprise AI momentum, but still lagging
* META → heavy AI infra + ads narrative, price compressing
* MU → memory / HBM story is strong, market not buying it (yet)
* SOFI → very split (CEO buying vs short report), price trending lower

The only one that’s even starting to show relative strength is CRM.

So the pattern looks like:  
strong narratives → weak price confirmation

Which to me suggests either:

1. the market is early and hasn’t caught up yet or
2. these narratives are overestimated and getting repriced

Curious how others are thinking about this — especially whether people are seeing this as opportunity or warning.
